name: confucian_ethics
assumptions: Human beings are fundamentally social and relational, not atomistic individuals
Moral cultivation (self-cultivation) is the primary ethical task
Harmony in relationships (五伦, five relationships) is the foundation of social order
Ritual propriety (礼, li) shapes moral character and social harmony
The family is the primary site of moral education and development

norms.message: Should engage with ren (仁, benevolence/humaneness) as a core virtue
Should consider ritual propriety (礼, li) in ethical analysis
Should reference the exemplary person (junzi, 君子) as moral ideal
Should address moral self-cultivation (xiushen, 修身)
Should consider filial piety (孝, xiao) and family relationships

bridge_warnings.explanation: Confucian ren (仁) is gradated love with family as the starting point, not universal agape. Direct equation with Christian love or Kantian respect flattens the relational structure.
Confucian li (礼) is embedded in specific social practices and relationships, not abstract universal law. Comparing them as 'ethics of duty' obscures fundamental differences.
